hyperedges.label: Channel adapters self-register via appended import into src/channels/index.ts barrel
OneCLI Agent Vault as the sole vault-only credential injection model across host, docs, and provider skills
Idempotent REMOVE.md skill-reversal pattern shared across channel/provider/tool skills

Chat SDK channel adapters (Google Chat, GitHub, Linear, Matrix, iMessage) share one install pattern: fetch the channels branch, copy the adapter + registration test, append a self-registration import to src/channels/index.ts, install a pinned adapter package, and validate via a registration test asserting the registry contains the channel and via createChatSdkBridge usage checked by the build.

The Gmail and Google Calendar MCP-tool skills share the OneCLI-native pattern: onecli-managed stub credential files, mcpServers + additionalMounts persisted to the central DB and regenerated into container.json via materializeContainerJson on every spawn, worked around missing add-mount CLI support via issue #2395.

The add-karpathy-llm-wiki skill, its copied llm-wiki.md idea document, and the three-layer LLM Wiki pattern jointly form the persistent-knowledge-base feature that gets instantiated per NanoClaw group.
Chat SDK channel adapter self-registration pattern shared across resend/slack/teams/telegram/webex/signal skills
OpenCode provider's four explicitly-guarded integration points: host/container barrels, SDK dependency, and Dockerfile install
rtk transparent Bash-command compression: binary mount + PreToolUse hook wired together in settings.json
Shared channel-install pattern: fetch from channels branch, copy adapter + registration test, wire self-registration import, pin+install package, build & vitest
OpenClaw-to-v2 migration workflow: main skill, cron-mapping reference doc, and its reversal
OneCLI vault as the destination for container-facing credentials across install/migration skills
PR lifecycle automation: template checkboxes drive auto-labeling, CI validates every PR, Qodo resolver fixes AI-flagged review issues
Agent memory persistence: per-group Wiki Memory writes on the generic memory-system template, safeguarded by a scheduled pre-shutdown review before the container is reaped
Shared PostHog diagnostics opt-in flow (identical api_key, show-JSON-then-ask, Yes/No/Never-again pattern) used by both migrate-nanoclaw and update-nanoclaw skills
Agent self-modification flow: skill decision tree over create_agent / install_packages / add_mcp_server / send_to_agent
AgentProvider interface implemented by Claude, Codex, and OpenCode providers
Inbound-to-outbound message pipeline: Chat SDK bridge, ChannelAdapter, agent-runner poll loop, architecture diagram depiction
Two-DB Session Split Pattern (single-writer inbound/outbound)
Skills-as-Customization Ecosystem (customizing + skills-model + skill-guidelines)
Approaches to Switching/Routing LLM Providers in NanoClaw
Container-detected limit → host fallback switch flow
Spec → Plan → Review → Fix lifecycle for LLM fallback feature
Pending-row + delivery + response-handler pattern shared by approvals, interactive, and self-mod modules

hyperedges.label: Channel adapters self-register via appended import into src/channels/index.ts barrel
OneCLI Agent Vault as the sole vault-only credential injection model across host, docs, and provider skills
Idempotent REMOVE.md skill-reversal pattern shared across channel/provider/tool skills

Chat SDK channel adapters (Google Chat, GitHub, Linear, Matrix, iMessage) share one install pattern: fetch the channels branch, copy the adapter + registration test, append a self-registration import to src/channels/index.ts, install a pinned adapter package, and validate via a registration test asserting the registry contains the channel and via createChatSdkBridge usage checked by the build.

The Gmail and Google Calendar MCP-tool skills share the OneCLI-native pattern: onecli-managed stub credential files, mcpServers + additionalMounts persisted to the central DB and regenerated into container.json via materializeContainerJson on every spawn, worked around missing add-mount CLI support via issue #2395.

The add-karpathy-llm-wiki skill, its copied llm-wiki.md idea document, and the three-layer LLM Wiki pattern jointly form the persistent-knowledge-base feature that gets instantiated per NanoClaw group.
Chat SDK channel adapter self-registration pattern shared across resend/slack/teams/telegram/webex/signal skills
OpenCode provider's four explicitly-guarded integration points: host/container barrels, SDK dependency, and Dockerfile install
rtk transparent Bash-command compression: binary mount + PreToolUse hook wired together in settings.json
Shared channel-install pattern: fetch from channels branch, copy adapter + registration test, wire self-registration import, pin+install package, build & vitest
OpenClaw-to-v2 migration workflow: main skill, cron-mapping reference doc, and its reversal
OneCLI vault as the destination for container-facing credentials across install/migration skills
PR lifecycle automation: template checkboxes drive auto-labeling, CI validates every PR, Qodo resolver fixes AI-flagged review issues
Agent memory persistence: per-group Wiki Memory writes on the generic memory-system template, safeguarded by a scheduled pre-shutdown review before the container is reaped
Shared PostHog diagnostics opt-in flow (identical api_key, show-JSON-then-ask, Yes/No/Never-again pattern) used by both migrate-nanoclaw and update-nanoclaw skills
Agent self-modification flow: skill decision tree over create_agent / install_packages / add_mcp_server / send_to_agent
AgentProvider interface implemented by Claude, Codex, and OpenCode providers
Inbound-to-outbound message pipeline: Chat SDK bridge, ChannelAdapter, agent-runner poll loop, architecture diagram depiction
Two-DB Session Split Pattern (single-writer inbound/outbound)
Skills-as-Customization Ecosystem (customizing + skills-model + skill-guidelines)
Approaches to Switching/Routing LLM Providers in NanoClaw
Container-detected limit → host fallback switch flow
Spec → Plan → Review → Fix lifecycle for LLM fallback feature
Pending-row + delivery + response-handler pattern shared by approvals, interactive, and self-mod modules
